message:
fix(ui): resolve hydration mismatches and improve UX interactions

Fixes React hydration issues and improves user interactions:

## useLocalStorage.ts

Simplified hydration logic to prevent SSR/client mismatches:
- Before: Used useState initializer to read localStorage, then
  queueMicrotask for hydration sync
- After: Initialize with `initialValue`, read localStorage in useEffect

This ensures server-rendered HTML matches initial client render,
preventing React hydration warnings. The localStorage value is
applied after mount, which is the correct pattern for SSR apps.

## DomainSelector.tsx

Fixed Atom icon rendering:
- Before: "Atom" case incorrectly rendered Zap as fallback
- After: Imports and uses actual Atom icon from lucide-react

## HypothesisArena.tsx

Improved test result cell click behavior:
- Before: Always set result to "supports" on click
- After: Cycles through result types on each click:
  pending → supports → challenges → eliminates → neutral → supports...

This provides intuitive inline editing without requiring a modal dialog.
Users can click repeatedly to cycle to desired result type.

## AgentTribunalPanel.tsx & ObjectionRegisterPanel.tsx

Fixed SSR-incompatible state initialization:
- Before: Called `loadObjectionStatuses(threadId)` in useState initializer
- After: Initialize with empty object, load in useEffect

The previous pattern caused hydration mismatches because localStorage
reads during SSR return different values than on client. Moving the
load to useEffect ensures consistent initial render.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
